title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
summary: Reed Jobs' venture firm Yosemite has grown to 17 employees and is capitalizing on patent expirations of blockbuster drugs while integrating AI into its biotech operations. The firm has experienced rapid expansion since its founding, positioning itself at the intersection of biotech innovation and artificial intelligence applications in the sector.
content: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs's words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

zh.title: 里德·乔布斯宁愿谈论治愈癌症，也不愿谈论他的姓氏
zh.summary: 里德·乔布斯的风险投资公司约塞米蒂已发展到17名员工，正在利用畅销药物的专利过期期限，同时将人工智能整合到其生物技术业务中。该公司自成立以来经历了快速扩张，将自己定位在生物技术创新和人工智能在该领域应用的交点。
